title: bursera longipedunculata
intro: Bursera longipedunculata, also known as the Mexican copal tree, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its resin, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Bursera longipedunculata include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant's resin include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
